title: Weekly Insights Review
section_path: Weekly Insights Review
summary:
Read here everything you need to know


The three things I would tell my clients this week
‘Providence protect idiots' Otto Von Bismarck once exclaimed. I'm never quite sure what the direct intentions of the Almighty are. Bismarck, a Prussian Prince and one of the great leaders of the 19th century, might have had a more direct line of communication with the powers that be than I do.

children.summary:
Following ten consecutive hikes that lifted borrowing costs to the highest level since September 2007, Fed opted to hold their benchmark rate unchanged this week at between 5 and 5.25 percent. Read More

children.title: Disappointment over Chinese stimulus measures continued to hover over markets
section_path: Disappointment over Chinese stimulus measures continued to hover over markets
children.section_path_text: Disappointment over Chinese stimulus measures continued to hover over markets
children.summary: A stronger USD also weighed on investor appetite.

children.title: Macro/Geopolitics, Commodity Markets, Freight and Bunker Markets
section_path: Macro/Geopolitics, Commodity Markets, Freight and Bunker Markets
children.section_path_text: Macro/Geopolitics, Commodity Markets, Freight and Bunker Markets
children.summary: Interest rates dominated the past week's news flow. After bringing the interest rate to the highest level since 2007, the Federal Reserve paused its current hiking cycle as US inflation showed signs of slowing.

children.title: This marks May as Iran's highest monthly crude/condensate exports total since sanctions were imposed in November 2018

children.section_path_text: This marks May as Iran's highest monthly crude/condensate exports total since sanctions were imposed in November 2018
children.summary:
Ιran exported over 1.5mbd of crude/condensate in May, a 200kbd increase m-o-m and 400kbd above the 12-month average. Read More

children.title: China's retail sales in May grew year-on-year
section_path: China's retail sales in May grew year-on-year
children.section_path_text: China's retail sales in May grew year-on-year
children.summary: As we discussed in Commodore's most recent Weekly China Report, China's retail sales in May grew year-on-year by 12.7%. This has come in lower than many expectations and has been disappointing to various pundits, but nevertheless this is strong growth.
